pr.title: Promotion: single-step confidence ladder — one accepted run can no longer buy full trust (option B, BHV-036)
pr.description:
## Summary

Closes the accumulation gap flagged during Finding 2: a single accepted evidence application could jump a capability `very_low → very_high` / `blocked → ready` because the confidence score is the raw success ratio (1/1 = 1.0). This PR rate-limits **upward** published-level transitions to one ladder rung per accepted application (design option B — promotion-side; the scoring formula is untouched, option A evidence-mass damping is staged as a separate follow-up PR).

In `EvidencePromotionEngine.apply()` (accept path):

```
raw_level  = _level_from_score(_compute_score(new factors))
effective, clamped = _clamp_level(prior.confidence_level, raw_level)
    # very_low → low → medium → high → very_high; up: max +1 rung; down: never clamped
report = confidence.create(..., level_override=effective)   # score stays the pure ratio
readiness = _readiness_from_level(effective)                # so blocked→ready can't happen in one hop
intake_record["promotion"] = {raw_level, effective_level, clamped}
```

Key mechanics:
- `CapabilityConfidenceEngine.create()` gains an optional validated `level_override` so the clamped level persists in the durable confidence store (no scoring redesign — `score` remains the unmodified ratio).
- `_readiness_from_level` (new, same implementation-local/Program-6 status as `_readiness_from_score`, identical threshold mapping) is used in `apply` and `_current_state`, so the clamped level round-trips instead of being recomputed away from the raw score.
- Asymmetric by design: 4 distinct accepted PASSes climb `low → medium → high → very_high`; one FAIL from very_high (4/5 = 0.8) drops to `high` immediately, `clamped: false`.
- Quarantined/rejected/duplicate applications never advance the ladder (unchanged behavior); the clamp is fully auditable via the intake record's new `promotion` field and the reason string.

Behavior change is logged as **BHV-036** in `docs/logs/behavior-change-ledger.md`; the `testing-evidence-promotion` skill checklist is updated in the same PR (steps 2 and new 13).

What did NOT change: score formula/doctrine, readiness thresholds, chain semantics, quality gate, quarantine/duplicate/conflict/stale handling, Revit behavior (2024 baseline unaffected; no live validation required).

Tests (tier 2 — targeted, per testing policy): `tests/test_evidence_promotion.py` (incl. 5 new ladder regressions), `tests/test_capability_confidence.py` (level_override persist + invalid-raises), `tests/test_evidence_summary.py`, plus `tests/test_atlas.py` / `tests/test_local_runner.py` / `tests/test_evidence_quality.py` / `tests/test_execution_chain_orchestrator.py` — 199 passed, 1 skipped; ruff clean. Full pytest intentionally skipped (runs in CI).

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Promotion: single-step confidence ladder (rate-limit upward level transitions)

One accepted evidence application can no longer jump a capability
very_low -> very_high / blocked -> ready. The published confidence level
rises at most one ladder rung per accepted application; drops are never
clamped. Raw vs effective level + clamped flag are recorded on the
intake record's promotion field. Score doctrine unchanged (still the
pure success ratio). BHV-036.

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
